vulnerablePopulations: Urban populations with limited sun exposure
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DXA T-score ≤ -2.5 per WHO criteria, SOBEMOM guidelines

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: limited DXA access, ~15-20% coverage in high-risk groups

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: DXA scanning per SOBEMOM guidelines, FRAX tool adaptation for Brazil
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Calcium + vitamin D supplementation via SUS, bisphosphonates for diagnosed cases, fall prevention programs
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited DXA availability outside major cities, underdiagnosis, vitamin D deficiency despite tropical climate
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: SOBEMOM national guidelines, SUS provision of basic medications (calcium, vitamin D, alendronate)
